briefing.headline: Go small, go slow — summer clear conditions demand finesse presentations.
briefing.gameplan:
In summer with clear water and pre frontal conditions, finesse presentations shine.

Johnston: On the St. Lawrence, fish an 80-yard stretch of bottom in about 40 feet targeting a pod of giant smallmouth. Catch one, then leave the spot and make a big run away before returning, because pressured deepwater smallmouth shut down after each catch.

Key adjustment for pre frontal: aggressive, faster retrieves — fish are feeding actively before the front.

primaryApproach.targets: Focus on grass, ledge, flat. Work the transitions and edges where structure changes.

alternateApproach.retrieve: Match the conditions — aggressive, faster retrieves — fish are feeding actively before the front.
alternateApproach.targets: Focus on grass, ledge, flat. Work the transitions and edges where structure changes.

proInsights.insight: Palaniuk: 'My offshore rotation goes: 1) deep-diving crankbait to cover water fast and fire up the school, 2) swimbait for a slower moving presentation once crankbait bite dies, 3) big flutter spoon for vertical reaction bites on fish seen on elec...

KVD: 'Summer bass suspend when temps peak, no current, and thermocline forms. See the thermocline as a consistent band on your graph — fish above it, none below. Schooling activity away from bottom and structure is a giveaway. Clouds of suspended ...
Wheeler: Best offshore spots included areas adjacent to grass lines, actual holes in the grass, and hard-bottomed shell beds in 8-35 feet of water.
Johnston: On the St. Lawrence, fish a mix of rock, sand, and weed in 15 feet or less. Hit as many spots as you can and hope the right ones bite.

briefing.depthStrategy: Pre-frontal summer bass feed aggressively. Fish split between shallow morning patterns (2-6ft) and offshore structure (12-25ft). Target grass lines, points, and ledges as falling pressure triggers movement.
adjustIf: If the front stalls and conditions stabilize, dial back aggression and focus on higher-percentage structure
If water clarity decreases from wind or rain, upsize lures and switch to brighter colors
If you get followers without commits, add scent or downsize your hook/weight
